items.situation: student asking about gym or sports facility hours
items.speaker: I thought the rec center stayed open later on Fridays.

options.A: Actually, they cut back the hours this term.
options.B: The new treadmills are really popular.
options.C: I left my gym bag in the locker room.
options.D: Yes, you can sign up for a class there.

items.explanation: 正确答案A以话语标记词"Actually"开头，间接纠正说话者的假设，说明本学期缩短了开放时间，自然地推进对话。B是语义联想陷阱（rec center→treadmills，只描述器材而不回应开放时间）。C是离题但语法正确的句子（在另一个场景说得通，但与开放时间无关）。D是答错问题类型（说话者没有问能否报名课程，而是关于开放时间的陈述）。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: Can I copy your notes from yesterday's lecture?

options.A: The lecture hall was freezing yesterday.
options.B: I'll just email you my whole folder.
options.C: Yes, I take notes in every class.
options.D: My notebook has a blue cover.

items.explanation: 正确答案B不直接回答"能不能复印"，而是通过解决潜在需求来回应——直接把整个文件夹发邮件给对方，这是context_shift范式。A是离题但语法正确（描述教室很冷，与借笔记无关）。C看似回答yes，但只是补充无用信息（"我每节课都记笔记"并未真正答应借出，是答错重点/自然对话陷阱）。D是语义联想陷阱（notes→notebook，只描述笔记本外观而不回应请求）。

items.situation: student asking a librarian about a book or resource
items.speaker: I can't seem to find this title on the shelves.

options.A: The shelves were just reorganized last week.
options.B: I love the new reading room upstairs.
options.C: Well, it might be checked out right now.
options.D: No, you can't reserve a study room here.

items.explanation: 正确答案C以话语标记词"Well"开头，间接给出书找不到的合理原因（可能被借走了），自然推进对话，属于marker_led_indirect范式。A是语义联想陷阱（shelves→重新整理书架，绕开了书的去向）。B是离题但语法正确（聊楼上的阅览室，与找书无关）。D是答错问题类型（说话者没有问能否预订自习室）。

items.situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend
items.speaker: How're we getting to the wedding on Saturday?

options.A: The highway is always busy on weekends.
options.B: Yes, I got a new car last month.
options.C: The wedding starts at four o'clock.

items.explanation: 正确答案D是地道的口语表达"Leave the driving to me"（交给我开就行），自然回应了怎么去的问题，属于idiomatic范式。A是语义联想陷阱（getting there→highway，只谈高速路况而不解决出行安排）。B是答错问题类型（问的是怎么去，不是yes/no关于车）。C是语义联想陷阱（wedding→婚礼时间，答错了方面，问的是怎么去而非几点）。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation
items.speaker: What's been bothering you all week?

options.A: Is it that obvious to everyone?
options.B: The week went by really fast.
options.C: I bought a new planner yesterday.
options.D: No, I haven't met her yet.

items.explanation: 正确答案A用一个反问"Is it that obvious to everyone?"来回应关切，承认确实有心事并推进对话，属于counter_question范式。B是语义联想陷阱（week→"这周过得很快"，重复关键词但不回应烦恼）。C是离题但语法正确（买了新记事本，与烦恼无关）。D是答错语境（用no回答，但针对的是完全不同的问题，关于是否见过某人）。

items.situation: student asking about a campus event or concert
items.speaker: Why was the jazz concert moved to the gym?

options.A: Jazz isn't really my kind of music.
options.B: The usual hall is being repainted.
options.C: The gym has brand new equipment.
options.D: No, I didn't buy a ticket yet.

items.explanation: 正确答案B直接回答了为什么改到体育馆——平时用的礼堂在重新粉刷，属于direct_topical范式。A是离题但语法正确（表达个人对爵士乐的偏好，不回答原因）。C是语义联想陷阱（gym→新器材，只描述体育馆而不回答"为什么改地点"）。D是答错问题类型（问的是why，不是关于是否买票的yes/no）。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: Don't we need a separate cover page for this?

options.A: The lab equipment was hard to set up.
options.B: Yes, I covered that topic last week.
options.C: Actually, a simple header at the top is fine.
options.D: The printer in the lab is out of toner.

items.explanation: 正确答案C以话语标记词"Actually"开头，间接回答不需要单独封面页，顶部一个简单标题即可，属于marker_led_indirect范式。A是离题但语法正确（谈实验设备难装，与封面页要求无关）。B是多义/误解陷阱（cover作"讲过某主题"的意思，而非"封面"）。D是语义联想陷阱（lab→打印机没墨，绕开了封面页问题）。

items.speaker: Where're we headed for the long weekend?

options.A: The weekend always feels too short.
options.B: I packed my bag last night.
options.C: No, I haven't booked anything yet.
options.D: How about the coast for a change?

items.explanation: 正确答案D用反问"How about the coast for a change?"提出一个具体的去处建议，推进对话，属于counter_question范式。A是语义联想陷阱（weekend→"周末总是太短"，重复关键词但不回答去哪）。B是离题但语法正确（昨晚收拾了行李，与去哪无关）。C是答错问题类型（问的是where，却用no回答关于是否预订）。
